content.full_text:
January 17, 1977


77-01-150

Franz E. Glen
50 Crestline Drive, Apt. 1
San Francisco, CA  94131

Dear Mr. Glen:

Thank you for your letter of January 5, 1977, requesting advice with respect to your reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act.  Based on the facts set forth in your letter, there are no additional reporting obligations imposed on you by virtue of your service as a trustee of the I.B.E.W. Local Union Pension Trust.  Next year when you are required to fill out a Form 721, you will be required to list the I.B.E.W. Local No. 6 Pension Trust as a source of income if you receive $250 or more from the trust in expenses or salary during the period covered by the statement.

If, as a member of the Coastal Commission, you wish to make or participate in the making of a governmental decision which would materially affect the I.B.E.W. Local No. 6 Pension Trust, I would be happy to discuss whether you should disqualify yourself from such a decision pursuant to the Political Reform Act.

If you have any further questions please feel free to contact me.

Sincerely,


Ted Prim
Staff Counsel
Legal Division

TP:jo

JAN 7 11:49AM '77

January 5, 1977

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento,California 95814

Attention: Mr. Ted Pinkman

Re: Franz E. Glen
50 Crestline Drive, Apt.1
San Francisco, Ca. 94131

Gentlemen:

Having been newly appointed to the North Central Coast Regional Commission, I have filed a Form 720 Statement of Economic Interests with the State Coastal Commission Office. I have noted on my Statement of Economic Interests by the attachment of a copy of this letter that in my employment as the Business Manager-Financial Secretary of I.B.E.W. Local Union No.6 in San Francisco, I also serve as one of the Trustees on the I.B.E.W. Local No.6 Pension Trust. I have met the requirements for a vested interest in that I.B.E.W. No.6 Pension.

The funds of the I.B.E.W. Local No.6 Pension Trust are presently invested in a large variety of stocks and bonds and the Trust also holds a $23,000.00 note on the property located at 1850 Mission Street, San Francisco,California.

Please advise me if I need to disclose any additional information in my capacity as a Pension Trustee.

Very truly yours,

Franz E. Glen

cc: Deputy Attorney General Dan Taaffe
Office of the Attorney General
350 McAllister Street
San Francisco,Ca. 94102
